COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CONTINUED TO JUNE 27, 2007, PER AGREEMENT OF PARTIES. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: NICHOLE RODICH, CSR 11604

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BRIGHT NOW! DENTAL, INC.'S MOTION TO LIFT DISCOVERY STAY AND CONTINUE HEARING ON OLSON'S ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DISCOVERY IS LIFTED TO ALLOW THE FOLLOWING DISCOVERY: 1) DEPOSITION OF OLSEN FOR NO MORE THAN SIX HOURS (EACH SIDE). LIMITED TO CAUSES OF ACTION INCLUDED IN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION; 2) DEPOSITION OF TAKENAGA FOR NO MORE THAN TWO HOURS (EACH SIDE); 3) DEPOSITION OF JOHNSON FOR NO MORE THAN TWO HOURS (EACH SIDE); 4) OLSON TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS IN RESPONSE TO REQUESTS 1, 3, 7, 12, 13, 39 AND 40, RELEVANT TO CLAIMS AT ISSUE; 5) OLSON TO RESPOND TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 1, 4, 6, AND 7. WRITTEN DISCOVERY AND DOCUMENTS TO BE PRODUCED BY JULY 11TH AND DEPOSITIONS TO TAKE PLACE ON JULY 16TH AND 17TH, PER AGREEMENT OF PARTIES; 6) REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS TO BE ANSWERED: 17, 21, 23, 34, 35, 36 AND 40; THE BALANCE ARE UNNECESSARY AT THIS TIME. ANTI-SLAPP MOTION CONTINUED TO AUGUST 9, 2007 WITH OPPOSITION DUE JULY 27, 2007 AND REPLY DUE AUGUST 3, 2007. THE JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND THE MOTION TO STRIKE CALENDARED ON JUNE 29, 2007 IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 9, 2007. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TERRY OLSON'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PURSUANT TO ANTI-SLAPP. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. DEFENDANT CANNOT MEET THE PUBLIC INTEREST REQUIREMENT. DUCHARME V INT'L, ETC. (2003) 110 CAL APP 4TH 107, 119; KURWA V HARRINGTON, ET AL. (2007) CAL APP 4TH 841. SECOND, THIS IS A COMMERCIAL DISPUTE AND THE RECORD ESTABLISHES THAT OLSEN HAS SIGNIFICANT INVOLVEMENT IN THE PELANDALE BUSINESS. THIRD, THE RECORD FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT OLSEN'S ALLEGED CONDUCT IS PROTECTED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Ntc Of Mtn And Special Mtn To Strike Pursuant To Anti Slapp
Off Calendar Sep-17-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TERRY OLSON'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PURSUANT TO ANTI-SLAPP.
APPEARANCES: RICHARD E. ELDER, III AND LEE KISSMAN, ESQUIRE (415.856.7032 ELDER) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JOHANNA CALABRIA, ESQUIRE (415.781.7400) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. DEFENDANT CANNOT MEET THE PUBLIC INTEREST REQUIREMENT. DUCHARME V INT'L, ETC. (2003) 110 CAL APP 4TH 107, 119; KURWA V HARRINGTON, ET AL. (2007) CAL APP 4TH 841. SECOND, THIS IS A COMMERCIAL DISPUTE AND THE RECORD ESTABLISHES THAT OLSEN HAS SIGNIFICANT INVOLVEMENT IN THE PELANDALE BUSINESS. THIRD, THE RECORD FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT OLSEN'S ALLEGED CONDUCT IS PROTECTED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Discovery Stay And Continue Hearing On Olson'S Anti-Slapp Motion
Off Calendar Jun-27-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF BRIGHT NOW! DENTAL, INC.'S MOTION TO LIFT DISCOVERY STAY AND CONTINUE HEARING ON OLSON'S ANTI-SLAPP MOTION.
APPEARANCES: RICHARD E. ELDER, III AND LEE KISSMAN, ESQUIRE (PAUL HASTINGS, 415.856.7032) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JOHANNA CALABRIA (CALABRIA LAW GROUP, 415.722.2992) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DISCOVERY IS LIFTED TO ALLOW THE FOLLOWING DISCOVERY: 1) DEPOSITION OF OLSEN FOR NO MORE THAN SIX HOURS (EACH SIDE). LIMITED TO CAUSES OF ACTION INCLUDED IN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION; 2) DEPOSITION OF TAKENAGA FOR NO MORE THAN TWO HOURS (EACH SIDE); 3) DEPOSITION OF JOHNSON FOR NO MORE THAN TWO HOURS (EACH SIDE); 4) OLSON TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS IN RESPONSE TO REQUESTS 1, 3, 7, 12, 13, 39 AND 40, RELEVANT TO CLAIMS AT ISSUE; 5) OLSON TO RESPOND TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 1, 4, 6, AND 7. WRITTEN DISCOVERY AND DOCUMENTS TO BE PRODUCED BY JULY 11TH AND DEPOSITIONS TO TAKE PLACE ON JULY 16TH AND 17TH, PER AGREEMENT OF PARTIES; 6) REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS TO BE ANSWERED: 17, 21, 23, 34, 35, 36 AND 40; THE BALANCE ARE UNNECESSARY AT THIS TIME. ANTI-SLAPP MOTION CONTINUED TO AUGUST 9, 2007 WITH OPPOSITION DUE JULY 27, 2007 AND REPLY DUE AUGUST 3, 2007. THE JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND THE MOTION TO STRIKE CALENDARED ON JUNE 29, 2007 IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 9, 2007. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
